line: constraint user_favorite_merchants.user_favorite_merchants_user_id_fkey: FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
reason: Identical FK definition; the hand-written migration used Postgres's default constraint name (…_fkey) while drizzle names it …_users_id_fk. Renaming a production constraint carries deploy risk for zero behavioural benefit.

reason: Pair of the …_fkey entry above — same FK, drizzle-generated name.

line: trigger pending_payouts.pending_payouts_emission_conservation: CREATE TRIGGER pending_payouts_emission_conservation BEFORE INSERT ON public.pending_payouts FOR EACH ROW WHEN (((new.kind = 'emission'::text) AND (new.state <> 'failed'::text))) EXECUTE FUNCTION assert_emission_conservation()
reason: Hardening A1/C10 emission-conservation fence (migration 0044) — drizzle's schema DSL cannot represent triggers; the hand-written migration is the source of truth. Enforces minted-net <= mirror balance at the DB boundary so no future writer path can re-open the unbacked-mint hole two cold audits flagged.

line: trigger pending_payouts.pending_payouts_mint_reentry_conservation: CREATE TRIGGER pending_payouts_mint_reentry_conservation BEFORE UPDATE OF state ON public.pending_payouts FOR EACH ROW WHEN (((old.state = 'failed'::text) AND (new.state <> 'failed'::text) AND (new.kind = ANY (ARRAY['emission'::text, 'order_cashback'::text, 'interest_mint'::text])))) EXECUTE FUNCTION assert_emission_conservation()
reason: Hardening A1/C10 re-entry conservation fence (migration 0044) — the second way a row enters the counted mint set is an admin payout-retry flipping failed → pending; without this trigger the freed headroom could be double-materialised (adversarial-review P0). Drizzle cannot represent triggers.
